meta.title: TimeToStop (TTS) — ASM withdrawal after paediatric epilepsy surgery
meta.authors: Lamberink HJ, Boshuisen K, Otte WM, Geleijns K, Braun KPJ, on behalf of the TimeToStop Study Group
meta.outcome: Individualised risk of seizure recurrence (2 and 5 years), chance of long-term seizure freedom, and chance of cure after withdrawal of antiseizure medication following epilepsy surgery in children.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. Three nomograms from the TimeToStop study (Lamberink et al., Epilepsia 2018) for children who became seizure-free after epilepsy surgery and are considering withdrawal of antiseizure medication. They estimate (1) the risk of seizure recurrence at 2 and 5 years after starting withdrawal, (2) the chance of long-term seizure freedom, and (3) the chance of cure (seizure-free and off medication).

Cohort & performance. 766 children who underwent epilepsy surgery in 15 centres across 8 European countries (the European retrospective TimeToStop study). Adjusted concordance statistic 0.68 (95% CI 0.67–0.69) for seizure recurrence and 0.73 (95% CI 0.72–0.75) for eventual seizure freedom.

Note. The three models use different predictor subsets, so a factor may add points to one score and not another. Values outside the published range are capped; risks beyond the table ends are reported as bounds (e.g. <2%, >54%).

Source & acknowledgement. This calculator is a faithful re-implementation of the Epilepsy Prediction Tools nomogram published by the TimeToStop (TTS) study group at UMC Utrecht. The point tables and risk look-ups are taken from the authors' own reference implementation (github.com/wmotte/epilepsypredictiontools, Apache-2.0), not digitised from the figures, and reproduce it exactly. Please cite the original paper and visit epilepsypredictiontools.info for the authors' original tool.

predictors.name: Age at withdrawal
predictors.assess: The child's age (years) when antiseizure-medication withdrawal is started. Older age at withdrawal adds points to both the recurrence and the cure nomograms.

predictors.name: Time to reduction (surgery → start of withdrawal)
predictors.assess: Months from epilepsy surgery until ASM withdrawal is started. Very early withdrawal carries the most points; the effect flattens and then rises slightly again after ~2.5 years. Only affects the recurrence nomogram.

predictors.name: Preoperative MRI
predictors.assess: MRI appearance before surgery. Only a multifocal MRI adds points (to the recurrence nomogram).

predictors.name: Completeness of anatomical lesion resection
predictors.assess: Whether the anatomical lesion was completely resected. An incomplete resection adds points to all three nomograms.

predictors.name: Daily seizure frequency before surgery
predictors.assess: Seizures per day before surgery (cap at 24 if higher). The relationship is U-shaped: points are lowest around 5–7 seizures/day and rise for both very low and very high frequencies. Only affects the long-term-freedom nomogram.

predictors.name: Number of ASMs at time of surgery
predictors.assess: How many antiseizure medications the child was taking at the time of surgery. More drugs add points to the long-term-freedom and cure nomograms.

predictors.name: EEG before surgery
predictors.assess: Preoperative EEG. Only interictal epileptiform discharges add points (to the recurrence and cure nomograms).

predictors.name: Aetiology
predictors.assess: Underlying cause of the epilepsy. Only affects the cure nomogram — Rasmussen encephalitis carries by far the most points, a vascular lesion none.

model.panel_title: Outcome after ASM withdrawal following paediatric epilepsy surgery
model.note: Three separate nomograms with different predictor subsets. Population-level estimates — always apply clinical judgement.
model.viz_info: Each outcome is shown as a pie: the coloured slice is the predicted chance. Below, each factor's points are broken down separately for the three nomogram scores (recurrence, long-term freedom, cure).

provenance.source: Lamberink et al., Epilepsia 2018;59(3):e28-e33 (doi 10.1111/epi.14020, PMID 29446447). Point tables and risk look-ups taken verbatim from the authors' reference implementation at github.com/wmotte/epilepsypredictiontools (docs/js/tts-calc.js, Apache-2.0), as published at epilepsypredictiontools.info.
provenance.notes: Three nomogram totals. m1 (recurrence) = age-at-withdrawal + time-to-reduction + MRI + EEG + completeness. m2 (long-term freedom) = seizure frequency + n-ASMs + completeness. m3 (cure) = aetiology + age-at-withdrawal + EEG + n-ASMs + completeness. Seizure frequency uses the authors' non-uniform 40-step value list (0-24/day) via the slider `vals` mechanism, preserving their exact nearest-value snapping.
